mappings.rationale: Directly covers individual behavioural activities (food getting, locomotion, aggression), social behaviour, and animal communication.
Covers associative learning, classical and operant conditioning, and complex problem solving as higher behavioural capacities.
Addresses the neural basis of learning, memory, reasoning, and the brain structures underlying complex behavioural capacities.
Covers the neural mechanisms of learning, perception, spatial cognition, and complex problem solving in animals.
Addresses developmental aspects of learning including imprinting, language learning, and the development of reasoning capacities.
Covers group behaviour dynamics, social communication, and the range of social behaviour among social animals.
Directly covers habituation, sensitization, associative learning, classical conditioning, operant conditioning, and observational learning.
Addresses complex problem solving, insight, reasoning, and discriminations of relational and abstract stimuli.
Covers spatial learning, maze learning, and the neural basis of memory formation underlying behavioural capacities.
Addresses complex problem solving, generalized rule learning, and the cognitive capacities underlying higher behavioural functions.
Covers perceptual learning and observational learning, relevant to how animals develop and expand their behavioural repertoire.
Covers insect social behaviour, communication systems, and the dynamics of social behaviour in insect colonies.
Illustrates the full range of mammalian behavioural capacities from basic food getting to complex social structures.
Directly relevant to classical conditioning, a foundational form of associative learning in the study of animal behaviour.
